KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, To Philemon our dear friend and co-worker,
text.Rotherham: Paul, prisoner of Christ Jesus, and Timothy my brother,—unto Philemon the beloved, and a fellow worker of ours,
text.KJV: Paul, a prisoner of Jesus Christ, and Timothy our brother, unto Philemon our dearly beloved, and fellowlabourer,
text.NABRE: Address and Greeting. Paul, a prisoner for Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, to Philemon, our beloved and our co-worker,
text.DRB: Paul the prisoner of Christ Jesus, and brother Timothee: to Philemon the beloved and our coadjutor,

text.NRSV: to Apphia our sister, to Archippus our fellow soldier, and to the church in your house:
text.Rotherham: And unto Apphia our sister. And unto Archippus our fellow-soldier, and unto the assembly which meeteth, at thy house:
text.KJV: And to our beloved Apphia, and Archippus our fellowsoldier, and to the church in thy house:
text.NABRE: to Apphia our sister, to Archippus our fellow soldier, and to the church at your house.
text.DRB: and to Appia our dearest sister, and to Archippus our fellow-soldier and to the church which is in thy house.

text.NRSV: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Favour unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: Grace to you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: Grace to you and peace from God our Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: When I remember you in my prayers, I always thank my God
text.Rotherham: I am giving thanks unto my God—always, making mention, of thee, in my prayers,
text.KJV: I thank my God, making mention of thee always in my prayers,
text.NABRE: Thanksgiving. I give thanks to my God always, remembering you in my prayers,
text.DRB: I give thanks to my God, always making a memory of thee in my prayers,

text.NRSV: because I hear of your love for all the saints and your faith toward the Lord Jesus.
text.Rotherham: Hearing of thy love, and of the faith which thou hast towards the Lord Jesus and towards all the saints,—
text.KJV: Hearing of thy love and faith, which thou hast toward the Lord Jesus, and toward all saints;
text.NABRE: as I hear of the love and the faith you have in the Lord Jesus and for all the holy ones,
text.DRB: hearing thy charity & faith which thou hast in our Lord Jesus, and toward all the saints:

text.NRSV: I pray that the sharing of your faith may become effective when you perceive all the good that we may do for Christ.
text.Rotherham: To the end that, the fellowship of thy faith, may become, energetic, by a personal knowledge of every good thing that is in us towards Christ;
text.KJV: That the communication of thy faith may become effectual by the acknowledging of every good thing which is in you in Christ Jesus.
text.NABRE: so that your partnership in the faith may become effective in recognizing every good there is in us that leads to Christ.
text.DRB: that the communication of thy faith may be made evident in the agnition of all good that is in you in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: I have indeed received much joy and encouragement from your love, because the hearts of the saints have been refreshed through you, my brother.
text.Rotherham: For, much joy, have I had, and encouragement, by occasion of thy love, in that, the tender affections of the saints, have had rest given them by thee, brother.
text.KJV: For we have great joy and consolation in thy love, because the bowels of the saints are refreshed by thee, brother.
text.NABRE: Plea for Onesimus. For I have experienced much joy and encouragement from your love, because the hearts of the holy ones have been refreshed by you, brother.
text.DRB: For I have had great joy and consolation in thy charity, because the bowels of the saints have rested by thee brother.

text.NRSV: For this reason, though I am bold enough in Christ to command you to do your duty,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, though I have, much, freedom of speech, in Christ, to be enjoining upon thee that which is befitting,
text.KJV: Wherefore, though I might be much bold in Christ to enjoin thee that which is convenient,
text.NABRE: Therefore, although I have the full right in Christ to order you to do what is proper,
text.DRB: For the which thing having great confidence in Christ Jesus to command thee that which pertaineth to the purpose:

text.NRSV: yet I would rather appeal to you on the basis of love—and I, Paul, do this as an old man, and now also as a prisoner of Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: Yet, for loves sake, I rather exhort,—being, such a one, as Paul the aged, now, also, even a prisoner of Christ Jesus,—
text.KJV: Yet for love’s sake I rather beseech thee, being such an one as Paul the aged, and now also a prisoner of Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: I rather urge you out of love, being as I am, Paul, an old man, and now also a prisoner for Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: for charity rather I beseech, whereas thou art such an one, as Paul being old and now prisoner also of Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: I am appealing to you for my child, Onesimus, whose father I have become during my imprisonment.
text.Rotherham: I exhort thee concerning my own child, whom I have begotten in my bonds,—Onesimus:
text.KJV: I beseech thee for my son Onesimus, whom I have begotten in my bonds:
text.NABRE: I urge you on behalf of my child Onesimus, whose father I have become in my imprisonment,
text.DRB: I beseech thee for my son whom I have begotten in bands, Onesimus,

text.NRSV: Formerly he was useless to you, but now he is indeed useful both to you and to me.
text.Rotherham: Him who, at one time, was, unto thee, unserviceable, but, now, unto thee and unto me, serviceable;
text.KJV: Which in time past was to thee unprofitable, but now profitable to thee and to me:
text.NABRE: who was once useless to you but is now useful to [both] you and me.
text.DRB: who hath been sometime unprofitable to thee, but now profitable both to me and thee,

text.NRSV: I am sending him, that is, my own heart, back to you.
text.Rotherham: Whom I have sent back unto thee—him, that is, my own, tender affections!—
text.KJV: Whom I have sent again: thou therefore receive him, that is, mine own bowels:
text.NABRE: I am sending him, that is, my own heart, back to you.
text.DRB: whom I have sent back to thee. And do thou receive him as mine own bowels.

text.NRSV: I wanted to keep him with me, so that he might be of service to me in your place during my imprisonment for the gospel;
text.Rotherham: Whom, I, was minded, with myself, to detain, that, in thy behalf, unto me, he might be ministering in the bonds of the joyful message;
text.KJV: Whom I would have retained with me, that in thy stead he might have ministered unto me in the bonds of the gospel:
text.NABRE: I should have liked to retain him for myself, so that he might serve me on your behalf in my imprisonment for the gospel,
text.DRB: Whom I would have retained with me, that for thee he might minister to me in the bands of the Gospel:

text.NRSV: but I preferred to do nothing without your consent, in order that your good deed might be voluntary and not something forced.
text.Rotherham: But, apart from thy mind, I wished to do, nothing, that, not as by necessity, thy goodness should be, but, by choice.
text.KJV: But without thy mind would I do nothing; that thy benefit should not be as it were of necessity, but willingly.
text.NABRE: but I did not want to do anything without your consent, so that the good you do might not be forced but voluntary.
text.DRB: but without thy counsel I would do nothing: that thy good might be not as it were of necessity, but voluntary.

text.NRSV: Perhaps this is why he was separated from you for a while, so that you might have him back forever,
text.Rotherham: For, peradventure, for this cause, was he separated for an hour, that, as an age-abiding possession, thou mightest have him back,—
text.KJV: For perhaps he therefore departed for a season, that thou shouldest receive him for ever;
text.NABRE: Perhaps this is why he was away from you for a while, that you might have him back forever,
text.DRB: For perhaps therefore he departed for a season from thee, that thou mightest take him again for ever.

text.NRSV: no longer as a slave but more than a slave, a beloved brother—especially to me but how much more to you, both in the flesh and in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: No longer as a servant, but above a servant—a brother beloved, very greatly to me, but, how much rather, to thee—both in the flesh and in the Lord!
text.KJV: Not now as a servant, but above a servant, a brother beloved, specially to me, but how much more unto thee, both in the flesh, and in the Lord?
text.NABRE: no longer as a slave but more than a slave, a brother, beloved especially to me, but even more so to you, as a man and in the Lord.
text.DRB: Now not as a servant, but for a servant, a most dear brother, especially to me, but how much more to thee both in the flesh and in our Lord?

text.NRSV: So if you consider me your partner, welcome him as you would welcome me.
text.Rotherham: If, therefore, thou holdest me as one in thy fellowship, take him unto thee, as myself;
text.KJV: If thou count me therefore a partner, receive him as myself.
text.NABRE: So if you regard me as a partner, welcome him as you would me.
text.DRB: If therefore thou take me for thy fellow; receive him as myself.

text.NRSV: If he has wronged you in any way, or owes you anything, charge that to my account.
text.Rotherham: And, if he hath wronged thee at all or oweth thee aught, the same, unto me, do thou reckon:—
text.KJV: If he hath wronged thee, or oweth thee ought, put that on mine account;
text.NABRE: And if he has done you any injustice or owes you anything, charge it to me.
text.DRB: And if he hath hurt thee any thing or is in thy debt, that impute to me.

text.NRSV: I, Paul, am writing this with my own hand: I will repay it. I say nothing about your owing me even your own self.
text.Rotherham: I, Paul, have written [it] with, my own, hand,—I, will repay [it]; that I may not tell thee—that, thyself, unto me, thou still owest.
text.KJV: I Paul have written it with mine own hand, I will repay it: albeit I do not say to thee how thou owest unto me even thine own self besides.
text.NABRE: I, Paul, write this in my own hand: I will pay. May I not tell you that you owe me your very self.
text.DRB: I Paul have written with mine own hand: I will repay it: not to say to thee, that thou owest me thine own self also.

text.NRSV: Yes, brother, let me have this benefit from you in the Lord! Refresh my heart in Christ.
text.Rotherham: Yea! brother, I, would, from thee, have help, in the Lord: give rest unto my tender affections in Christ.
text.KJV: Yea, brother, let me have joy of thee in the Lord: refresh my bowels in the Lord.
text.NABRE: Yes, brother, may I profit from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in Christ.
text.DRB: Yea brother. God grant I may enjoy thee in our Lord. Refresh my bowels in our Lord.

text.NRSV: Confident of your obedience, I am writing to you, knowing that you will do even more than I say.
text.Rotherham: Confident of thine obedience, I have written unto thee, knowing that, even beyond what I say, thou wilt do:—
text.KJV: Having confidence in thy obedience I wrote unto thee, knowing that thou wilt also do more than I say.
text.NABRE: With trust in your compliance I write to you, knowing that you will do even more than I say.
text.DRB: Trusting in thy obedience I have written to thee, knowing that thou wilt do above that also which I do say.

text.NRSV: One thing more—prepare a guest room for me, for I am hoping through your prayers to be restored to you.
text.Rotherham: At the same time, moreover, be also getting ready for me, a lodging; for I am hoping that, through your prayers, I shall be granted as a favour unto you.
text.KJV: But withal prepare me also a lodging: for I trust that through your prayers I shall be given unto you.
text.NABRE: At the same time prepare a guest room for me, for I hope to be granted to you through your prayers.
text.DRB: And withal provide me also a lodging. For I hope by your prayers that I shall be given to you.

text.NRSV: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, sends greetings to you,
text.Rotherham: There salute thee—Epaphras my fellow-captive in Christ Jesus,
text.KJV: There salute thee Epaphras, my fellowprisoner in Christ Jesus;
text.NABRE: Final Greetings. Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you,
text.DRB: There salute thee Epaphras my fellow-prisoner in Christ Jesus,

text.NRSV: and so do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my co-workers.
text.Rotherham: Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, Luke,—my fellow-workers.
text.KJV: Marcus, Aristarchus, Demas, Lucas, my fellowlabourers.
text.NABRE: as well as Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my co-workers.
text.DRB: Mark, Aristarchus, Demas and Luke my coadjutors.

text.NRSV: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
text.Rotherham: The favour of the Lord Jesus Christ, be with your spirit.
text.KJV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen. Written from Rome to Philemon, by Onesimus a servant.
text.NABRE: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
text.DRB: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
